tenders.title: Consultancy Services for Preparation of Basic Engineering and Depth Feasibility Study for Shipbuilding Cluster in Tuticorin, Tamil Nadu
tenders.estimatedValueLabel: Not disclosed in RFP body (feasibility for ~2,500-acre National Mega Shipbuilding Cluster)
tenders.scopeSummary: Selection of a consultant to prepare a Basic Engineering and Depth Feasibility Report with a conceptual plan for a National Mega Shipbuilding Cluster near VOC Port, Tuticorin (~2,500 acres), being developed by VOCPA with SIPCOT via an SPV. Scope covers land/waterfront assessment, dredging assessment, dock technology (floating/fixed dry dock), ship-repair facility, capital and operational cost estimates and depth feasibility analysis, aligned to Maritime India Vision 2030 / Amrit Kaal Vision 2047.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: At least one completed port project (Basic Engineering & Depth Feasibility / DPR / Detailed Engineering) for a shipbuilding-yard project of estimated capital cost >= Rs 1000 Cr in a single project, OR two port projects (Feasibility/DPR/Detailed Engineering) each of estimated capital cost >= Rs 700 Cr, in the last 7 years. An 'Eligible Assignment' means a port-facilities development project of capital cost >= Rs 500 Cr.
keyPersonnel: Port Sector Expert cum Team Leader (Civil grad/PG; led feasibility/DPR/Detailed Engineering in min. 3 port-sector eligible assignments, preferably greenfield)
Technical Expert (Civil) - marine/marine structures (7 yrs; >= 3 eligible assignments)
Technical Expert (Mechanical) - port equipment (7 yrs; >= 3 eligible assignments)
other: Positive net worth in FY 2024-25; valid registration and tax registrations; not blacklisted.
JV/Consortium max 4 members; Lead Member must meet >= 50% of turnover & experience criteria (>= 1 port DPR/Detailed Engineering project of capital cost >= Rs 700 Cr).
Each Key Personnel must score >= 60%; proposal rejected if Team Leader < 60% or any two other Key Personnel < 60%.
No Key Personnel above 75 years of age.

subCriteria: 70% of marks for one Eligible Assignment
technicalCriteria.notes: Eligible Assignment = port-facilities project of capital cost >= Rs 500 Cr.

technicalCriteria.criterion: Relevant experience of the Key Personnel
technicalCriteria.notes: Each CV scored on overall experience (bands, e.g. TL >25 yrs = 100%, up to 15 yrs = 80%) and years with current employer (2-5 yrs = 100%, 1-2 yrs = 50%).

tenders.sourceDocument: VOCPA RFP - Consultancy for Basic Engineering & Depth Feasibility Study, Shipbuilding Cluster, Tuticorin (application window 16.03.2026-15.04.2026)

tenders.title: Transaction Advisory Services for PPP Projects at Paradip Port
tenders.issuingAuthority: Paradip Port Authority (PPA) / Ministry of Shipping
tenders.estimatedValueLabel: Refer document (EMD Rs 1,00,000; tender fee Rs 10,000)
tenders.scopeSummary: Appointment of a transaction advisor to provide legal, technical and financial advisory for structuring and awarding PPP projects at Paradip Port (berth/terminal development on BOT/DBFOT and captive modes). Scope typically covers feasibility/business case, PPP structuring, RFQ/RFP drafting, bid process management and concession agreement finalisation. Contract duration 365 days; two-cover (technical + BOQ) submission.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: Per RFP (transaction advisory / PPP structuring experience in ports/infrastructure; DEA/IPA transaction-advisor style panels commonly referenced).
other: Two-cover system: (i) Fee/PreQual/Technical, (ii) Finance (BOQ).
EMD Rs 1,00,000; tender fee Rs 10,000; bid validity 180 days.

tenders.sourceDocument: PPA/MoS e-Tender Call Notice - Transaction Advisory Services for PPP Projects at Paradip Port

tenders.title: Selection of Transaction Advisor for Development of New Outer Harbour at Chennai Port on DBFOT Basis
tenders.scopeSummary: Selection of a transaction advisor to carry out the feasibility study and structure the ~Rs 8,000-10,000 Cr New Outer Harbour project at Chennai Port on a DBFOT/Hybrid-Annuity PPP basis (breakwater, reclamation, berths, container yard, connectivity, dredging, navigational aids). Scope covers feasibility, financial model, PPP structuring and bid process management for concessionaire selection. Assignment period ~532 days; RITES Ltd was subsequently appointed.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: Per RFP (transaction advisory / feasibility for large PPP port or infrastructure projects).
other: Limited tender (empanelled advisors); two-cover; bid validity 120 days.
Independent External Monitors appointed for the procurement.

tenders.sourceDocument: ChPA RFP T/MP3/25/2025/E - Transaction Advisor for New Outer Harbour (DBFOT); reported by The Hindu

tenders.title: Preparation of Techno-Economic Feasibility Report (TEFR) and Detailed Project Report (DPR) for New Port near Bahuda Muhan, Ganjam District, Odisha
tenders.estimatedValueLabel: Refer document (EMD Rs 5,00,000; tender fee Rs 10,000)
tenders.scopeSummary: Appointment of a consultant to prepare a Techno-Economic Feasibility Report (TEFR) followed by a Detailed Project Report (DPR) for a new (greenfield) port near Bahuda Muhan, Ganjam District, Odisha. Scope covers traffic/market study, marine and geotechnical surveys, port layout and marine works, dredging, connectivity, financial viability and DPR. Two-cover (technical + financial) submission; bid validity 180 days.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: Per RFP (prior port/maritime TEFR/DPR experience; work completion certificates required).
other: Procured in 'Works' category (Civil & Architectural / Consultancy services).
EMD Rs 5,00,000; tender fee Rs 10,000.

tenders.sourceDocument: MoS/PPA e-TCN - TEFR & DPR for New Port near Bahuda Muhan, Ganjam, Odisha

tenders.title: Appointment of Consultant for Preparation of Preliminary Feasibility Report for Betul Port
tenders.scopeSummary: Appointment of a consultancy firm with marine infrastructure expertise to prepare a Preliminary Feasibility Report for a satellite port at Betul (mouth of river Sal), Goa. Seven activities over a 3-month window: project initiation/site visits & data collection, market study, physical surveys (topography, bathymetry, sub-bottom profiling), layout/reclamation & connectivity assessment, Social Impact Assessment, block cost estimation, and the Preliminary Project Report. Lump-sum services contract; bid validity 120 days.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: Reputed consultancy firms with marine infrastructure expertise; audited P&L / turnover certificate and relevant work orders with completion certificates required per RFP.
other: Two-cover (technical + BOQ); lump-sum contract; 3-month completion.
Budgetary/feasibility stage; a separate competitive tender to follow for implementation.

tenders.sourceDocument: MPA tender CE/20/2026 - Preliminary Feasibility Report for Betul Port (reported by The Goan)

tenders.title: Consultancy Services for EIA Studies and Environmental & CRZ Clearances for Capital Dredging (Deepening of Channels to 16.0 m Draft) at ICTT
tenders.estimatedValueLabel: Refer document (EMD Rs 50,000; tender fee Rs 885)
tenders.scopeSummary: Appointment of a consultant to carry out Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) studies and obtain Environmental and Coastal Regulation Zone (CRZ) clearances for capital dredging to deepen the channels for vessels up to 16.0 m draft at the International Container Transhipment Terminal (ICTT), Cochin. Scope covers baseline environmental/marine studies, EIA/EMP, public consultation support, and preparation/processing of statutory clearance applications (MoEFCC/SEIAA/CZMA). Lump-sum, 180-day contract.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: Per RFP (accredited EIA consultant, typically NABET/QCI-accredited; prior EIA/CRZ clearance experience for ports/dredging/coastal projects).
other: Limited tender; two-cover (technical .pdf + price .xls).
EMD Rs 50,000; tender fee Rs 885; bid validity 180 days.

tenders.sourceDocument: CoPA tender T15/2090/2026-C - EIA/CRZ consultancy for capital dredging at ICTT

tenders.title: Engagement of Consultant for Techno-Economic Feasibility Study for Development of a Special Economic Zone on Reclaimed Land at Haldia Dock Complex
tenders.issuingAuthority: Syama Prasad Mookerjee Port, Kolkata (SMP - Haldia Dock Complex)
tenders.estimatedValueLabel: Refer document (EMD Rs 25,000; tender fee Rs 1,770)
tenders.scopeSummary: Engagement of a consultant (from shortlisted firms) to conduct a Techno-Economic Feasibility Study for developing a Special Economic Zone (SEZ) on land reclaimed from HFCL at the Haldia Dock Complex of Syama Prasad Mookerjee Port, Kolkata. Scope covers land use/market assessment, SEZ planning, techno-economic and financial viability, and implementation/PPP options. Limited tender; two-cover; lump-sum; bid validity 180 days.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: Per RFP; limited tender restricted to pre-shortlisted firms with relevant feasibility/SEZ/industrial-land planning experience.
other: Limited tender (shortlisted firms); EMD Rs 25,000 (exemption allowed); tender fee Rs 1,770.
Two covers: Techno-commercial proposal and Financial proposal.

tenders.sourceDocument: SMP Kolkata (HDC) RFP SDM-PE/RFP/9/2025-26 - TEFR for SEZ on reclaimed land at Haldia Dock Complex

tenders.title: Strategy Consulting Services to Formulate National Perspective Plan and Assess Feasibility for Developing One Mega Port on the East Coast for Handling Containers
tenders.estimatedValueLabel: Refer document (EMD Rs 2,00,000; tender fee Rs 10,000)
tenders.scopeSummary: Selection of a strategy consulting firm to formulate a National Perspective Plan and assess the feasibility of developing one mega (transhipment/gateway) container port on India's East Coast. Scope covers container-trade and hinterland demand analysis, benchmarking against global hubs, site/location assessment, techno-economic feasibility, and a strategic roadmap for capacity augmentation. Open tender; two-cover; bid validity 120 days.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: Per RFP (national/sector strategy and port/maritime feasibility experience; reputed strategy/transaction-advisory firms).
other: Open tender; two-cover (Fee/PreQual/Technical + Finance).
EMD Rs 2,00,000 (BG/ST exemption allowed); tender fee Rs 10,000.

tenders.sourceDocument: IPA RFP IPA/PP/NPP/2026 - Strategy Consulting for National Perspective Plan & East Coast mega port feasibility

tenders.title: Appointment of Project Monitoring Unit (PMU) for Dugarajapatnam Major Port cum Shipbuilding cum Ship Repair Cluster (EoI)
tenders.scopeSummary: Expression of Interest with budgetary offer from Tier-I consultancy firms empanelled with NICSI for appointment of a Project Monitoring Unit (PMU) providing project coordination, monitoring and advisory support for development of a Major Port cum Shipbuilding cum Ship Repair Cluster at Dugarajapatnam, Andhra Pradesh. The PMU deploys a minimum of three key personnel full-time at VPA for day-to-day coordination, DPR/bid-process and statutory-approval support.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: Experience in PMU / Transaction Advisory / Project Management for large infrastructure projects; experience in ports, maritime, shipbuilding, ship repair or industrial clusters preferred.
keyPersonnel: Team Lead cum Infrastructure & Coordination Expert (B.E./B.Tech Civil or B.Plan/M.Plan/MBA; 7 yrs overall, >= 4 yrs in port/maritime industrial projects, >= 3 yrs government consulting/advisory)
Two additional key personnel (2-3 yrs in logistics/port/maritime industrial projects; major ports / state maritime boards / govt maritime agency experience preferred)
other: Consultant must be a Tier-I firm empanelled with NICSI.
Minimum 3 key personnel deployed full-time at VPA office throughout the contract.

tenders.evaluationMethod: EoI + budgetary offer (leading to QCBS RFP)
tenders.sourceDocument: VPA EoI - PMU for Dugarajapatnam Major Port cum Shipbuilding cum Ship Repair Cluster

tenders.title: Appointment of a Technical Consultancy Firm for Preparation of Detailed Project Report (DPR) for Development of Dugarajapatnam Port-cum-Ship Building-cum-Ship Repair Cluster, Andhra Pradesh
tenders.issuingAuthority: Visakhapatnam Port Authority (VPA) / Andhra Pradesh Maritime Board (APMB)
tenders.scopeSummary: Appointment of a technical consultancy firm to prepare a Detailed Project Report for the development of the Dugarajapatnam Port-cum-Ship Building-cum-Ship Repair Cluster in Andhra Pradesh. Scope covers traffic/market studies, marine and geotechnical surveys, port and cluster master planning, marine works and dredging design, environmental studies, financial viability and DPR. Civil Engineering tender (two-cover).

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: Per RFP (port/maritime DPR and detailed-engineering experience for greenfield port / shipbuilding projects).
other: Follows the Dugarajapatnam PMU EoI; DPR consultancy floated May 2026 (closing 10.06.2026).

tenders.sourceDocument: VPA/APMB Civil Engineering tender No. 01/SE(S)/APMB/2026-27 - DPR for Dugarajapatnam Port cum Shipbuilding cum Ship Repair Cluster

tenders.title: Detailed Feasibility Study for Capacity Augmentation of National Waterway-1 and Detailed Engineering for its Ancillary Works
tenders.issuingAuthority: Inland Waterways Authority of India (IWAI), Ministry of Shipping (Jal Marg Vikas Project)
tenders.estimatedValueLabel: Est. ~₹45.4 Cr; awarded ₹18.81 Cr + GBP 0.74 million
tenders.scopeSummary: World Bank-financed consultancy for a Detailed Feasibility Study for capacity augmentation of National Waterway-1 (Ganga, Haldia-Varanasi/Allahabad) and detailed engineering for its ancillary works, including strategic planning, techno-economic feasibility, navigation infrastructure and terminal/lock design inputs. Selected under QCBS; contract awarded at Rs 188.1 Million + GBP 0.74 Million (excluding taxes).

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: Per World Bank REoI/RFP (inland waterway / marine feasibility & detailed engineering experience; international shortlist as contract exceeded US$0.8 million).
other: World Bank Guidelines (Selection & Employment of Consultants); QCBS with international shortlist; prior review.
One of a suite of IWAI/JMVP consultancies (ESIA/RAP, IWT sector strategy, ferry services, asset management, TSSC).

tenders.sourceDocument: World Bank Procurement Plan - Jal Marg Vikas Project (Capacity Augmentation of NW-1), Ref CANW-1/IWAI/JMV/1

tenders.title: Consultancy for Environmental & Social Impact Assessment (ESIA), Environmental Management Plan (EMP) and Resettlement Action Plan (RAP) for Capacity Augmentation of National Waterway-1
tenders.issuingAuthority: Inland Waterways Authority of India (IWAI), Ministry of Shipping (Jal Marg Vikas Project)
tenders.scopeSummary: World Bank-financed consultancy for ESIA, Environmental Management Plan and Resettlement Action Plan for the capacity-augmentation works on National Waterway-1 (Jal Marg Vikas). Scope covers environmental and social baseline studies, impact assessment, EMP and RAP for terminals, dredging and navigation works. Selected under QCBS; contract awarded at Rs 28.61 Million (excluding taxes).

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: Per World Bank REoI/RFP (environmental & social assessment experience for large infrastructure / waterway projects).
other: World Bank Guidelines; QCBS; prior review.
Companion assignment to the NW-1 Detailed Feasibility Study (CANW-1/IWAI/JMV/1).

tenders.sourceDocument: World Bank Procurement Plan - Jal Marg Vikas Project, Ref CANW-1/JMV/IWAI/2 (ESIA/EMP/RAP)

tenders.title: Selection of Consultant for Preparation of Techno-Economic Feasibility Report for Setting up of Multimodal Terminal / Dry Port in the Hinterland of Chennai Port
tenders.scopeSummary: RFP under the Sagarmala programme for selection of a consultant to prepare a Techno-Economic Feasibility Report for setting up a Multimodal Terminal / Dry Port in the hinterland of Chennai Port. Scope covers cargo/traffic demand assessment, site evaluation, multimodal connectivity, techno-economic and financial feasibility, and implementation/PPP options for port-led hinterland logistics infrastructure.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: Per RFP (feasibility/DPR experience in ports, logistics, multimodal terminals / dry ports).
other: Issued under the Sagarmala programme (port-led development / hinterland connectivity).

tenders.sourceDocument: Sagarmala/MoS RFP - TEFR for Multimodal Terminal / Dry Port in the hinterland of Chennai Port (2017)

tenders.title: Hiring of Strategy Project Management Unit (PMU) as part of the National Shipbuilding Mission (NSbM) Secretariat
tenders.issuingAuthority: Sagarmala Development Company Ltd (SDCL) / Sagarmala Finance Corporation Ltd (SMFCL)
tenders.scopeSummary: Selection of a Strategy Project Management Unit (PMU) to support the National Shipbuilding Mission (NSbM) Secretariat under SDCL/SMFCL. Scope covers strategy and programme management support, policy/scheme design, stakeholder coordination and monitoring for the shipbuilding/ship-repair ecosystem. Lump-sum consultancy of Rs 3.89 Cr over 365 days; two-cover submission.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: Per RFP (strategy / PMU / programme-management experience, preferably in maritime, shipbuilding or infrastructure sectors).
other: Open tender; two-cover (Fee/PreQual/Technical + Price Bid).
EMD Rs 5,00,000 (exemption allowed); tender fee Rs 59,000; bid validity 120 days.

tenders.sourceDocument: SDCL/SMFCL RFP SMFCL/PMU-NSBM/RFP/2026/67 - Strategy PMU for National Shipbuilding Mission

evaluationFramework.summary:
Consultant selection in India's ports, shipping and inland-waterways sector is governed by the General Financial Rules 2017 (Rule 192) and the Ministry of Finance 'Manual for Procurement of Consultancy & Other Services, 2022', and is overwhelmingly conducted through Quality and Cost Based Selection (QCBS). Because port/maritime feasibility, DPR, detailed-engineering, EIA/CRZ and transaction-advisory assignments are highly quality-sensitive, authorities weight technical merit heavily - most commonly 70:30 or 80:20 (GFR caps the non-financial weight at 80%). World Bank / multilateral-financed inland-waterway consultancies (e.g. IWAI's Jal Marg Vikas Project) follow the equivalent QCBS process under Bank guidelines. A two-envelope process is standard: proposals first clear mandatory eligibility (legal status, positive net worth, minimum average annual turnover over the last three financial years, similar-project experience, and - for major-port and PPP work - empanelment such as IPA / NICSI Tier-I / DEA transaction-advisor panels), then are scored on a 100-point technical scale, with only those meeting the minimum qualifying technical score (commonly 60% for many port RFPs, and 70-75% for larger or externally-financed assignments) having their financial bids opened.

The technical score is consistently built from four families of criteria, well illustrated by the VOCPA Tuticorin RFP (experience 30, methodology & work plan 20, key personnel 40, presentation 10, out of 100): (1) the firm's relevant port/maritime experience, measured by the number and capital value of comparable 'Eligible Assignments' - typically port-facilities projects of capital cost above a threshold such as Rs 500-1000 Cr, in the last 7-10 years; (2) the CVs of named key personnel by role (Team Leader / Port Planner, Marine/Marine-Structures Engineer, Mechanical/Port-Equipment Expert, Dredging/Hydrographic Expert, Environmental & CRZ Expert, Traffic/Commercial Expert, Financial/Transaction Advisor and Legal Expert), each scored on qualification and role-specific years of experience; (3) the proposed approach, methodology and work plan (understanding of the TOR, methodology and activity-wise work plan); and (4) a technical presentation to the evaluation committee. Key personnel are usually the single largest block (35-45% of technical marks), with the Team Leader most heavily weighted and often subject to a knock-out rule (the whole proposal fails if the Team Leader, or two or more other key experts, score below 60%). Firm experience typically carries 25-35%, while pure turnover marks are small even though the turnover threshold acts as a hard eligibility gate.

For a consultancy firm, the practical implication is that clearing eligibility (turnover, net worth, required empanelment and at least one/two qualifying port 'Eligible Assignments') is necessary but not sufficient; competitive scores come from the depth of directly comparable port/maritime references (especially high-value greenfield port, terminal, shipbuilding-cluster, dredging and waterway assignments), from fielding well-qualified, experienced named experts across all required disciplines, and from a genuinely project-specific methodology backed by a strong presentation. Firms should quantify their credentials against these four families - counts and values of similar assignments, and CV years by role - to estimate where they fall within the typical scoring bands.
evaluationFramework.dominantMethod: QCBS (commonly 80:20 for major-port advisory/feasibility; 70:30 also widely used; min technical score typically 60-75%)

criteria.label: Firm's Relevant Port / Maritime Experience
criteria.description: Number, capital value and relevance of comparable port/maritime consultancy 'Eligible Assignments' (feasibility/TEFR, DPR, detailed engineering, master planning, PMC, transaction advisory, EIA/CRZ, dredging/hydrographic supervision) completed for government/port clients, usually within the last 7-10 years. Authorities set an 'Eligible Assignment' threshold by project capital cost (e.g. >= Rs 500 Cr, with higher thresholds such as Rs 700-1000 Cr for the largest greenfield port / shipbuilding assignments) and award escalating marks for additional and higher-value assignments.

scoringGuidance.meaning: 3+ directly comparable port assignments above the RFP's value threshold (e.g. greenfield port / terminal / shipbuilding-cluster projects >= Rs 500-1000 Cr) in the last 7 years, spanning the required scope (feasibility/DPR/detailed engineering).
2 qualifying Eligible Assignments meeting the value/scope thresholds.
Exactly 1 qualifying Eligible Assignment - meets minimum eligibility but limited breadth (~70% of the experience marks in the VOCPA model).
No fully qualifying assignment, or only smaller/older/partly-comparable references - risks failing eligibility.

inputFields.label: Number of comparable port/maritime feasibility, DPR or detailed-engineering assignments completed (last 7-10 years)
inputFields.scoring: 0 = likely ineligible; 1 = fair (~70% of experience marks per VOCPA); 2 = good; 3+ = excellent (full marks). Each additional Eligible Assignment adds ~15% up to the cap.

inputFields.label: Capital cost of the largest port/maritime project your firm advised on (INR)
inputFields.scoring: Assignments above the RFP's Eligible-Assignment threshold count; common thresholds are Rs 500 Cr (general), Rs 700 Cr and Rs 1000 Cr (large greenfield / shipbuilding). Higher values qualify for more/larger RFPs.

inputFields.label: Number of assignments above the tender's Eligible-Assignment value threshold
inputFields.scoring: Directly maps to the experience marks (e.g. VOCPA: 70% for 1, +15% each additional, capped at 30 marks).

inputFields.label: Which port/maritime experience areas can you evidence?
inputFields.scoring: Covering more of the RFP's required areas raises marks; several RFPs require the qualifying assignment to match the specific scope (e.g. shipbuilding-yard feasibility, greenfield port DPR, EIA/CRZ for dredging).

criteria.label: Financial Strength & Standing (Turnover / Net Worth / Empanelment)
criteria.description: Average annual turnover from consultancy/professional services over the last three financial years, positive net worth, and (for major-port and PPP assignments) the required empanelment. This is primarily a hard eligibility gate (turnover thresholds range from ~Rs 5 Cr for mid-size feasibility/DPR work to much higher for large multi-year PMC/advisory work), often accompanied by a small number of scored marks.

scoringGuidance.meaning: Turnover well above the RFP threshold, positive net worth, and holds the required empanelment (IPA / NICSI Tier-I / DEA transaction-advisor panel where applicable).
Turnover comfortably above the minimum and net worth positive; empanelment held where relevant.
Turnover just meets the minimum threshold.
Turnover below the RFP minimum, negative net worth, or missing required empanelment - proposal is disqualified.

inputFields.label: Average annual turnover from consultancy services (last 3 FYs, INR)
inputFields.scoring: Must exceed the tender's minimum threshold (e.g. VOCPA feasibility RFP requires >= Rs 5 Cr average over 3 FYs). Larger PMC/advisory assignments set higher thresholds.

inputFields.label: Did your firm have positive net worth in the last financial year?
inputFields.scoring: Positive net worth (CA/SA-certified) is a common mandatory eligibility requirement; a negative net worth leads to rejection.

inputFields.label: Which relevant empanelment does your firm hold?
inputFields.scoring: Several major-port RFPs are restricted to IPA-empanelled or NICSI Tier-I firms; DEA-panel status helps for PPP transaction advisory. Lacking the specified empanelment can bar participation.

criteria.label: Key Personnel (CVs by Role)
criteria.description: Qualification and competence of named key experts, scored per role from their CVs (education, total years of experience, role-specific experience on eligible port assignments, and sometimes stability with the current employer). Typical roles: Team Leader / Port Planner, Marine / Marine-Structures Engineer, Mechanical / Port-Equipment Expert, Dredging / Hydrographic Expert, Environmental & CRZ Expert, Traffic / Commercial Expert, Financial / Transaction Advisor, and Legal Expert. The Team Leader is the most heavily weighted CV and is usually subject to a knock-out rule.

scoringGuidance.meaning: All required roles filled with experts exceeding minimum qualifications; Team Leader with 25+ years having led feasibility/DPR in 3+ eligible port assignments; specialists with direct port/maritime references.
All roles filled meeting minimums, with Team Leader and key specialists above the required experience floor.
Key roles filled at minimum thresholds; limited specialist depth or borderline experience years.
Missing key roles or CVs below minimums; Team Leader < 60% (or two other key experts < 60%) makes the whole proposal fail (VOCPA rule).

inputFields.label: Team Leader's total years of relevant port/maritime experience
inputFields.scoring: Bands reward seniority (e.g. VOCPA: >25 yrs = 100%, up to 15 yrs = 80% of the TL marks). Transaction-advisory RFPs often require ~15+ years in ports/EXIM-gateway infrastructure.

inputFields.label: Number of eligible port assignments the Team Leader has led (feasibility/DPR/detailed engineering)
inputFields.scoring: Team Leader typically must have led at least 3 port-sector eligible assignments (preferably greenfield); more comparable assignments score higher.

inputFields.label: Which specialist experts can you staff with qualifying CVs?
inputFields.scoring: Marks are allocated per role; filling every required specialist role at/above the minimum qualification and experience maximises the key-personnel block.

inputFields.label: Number of required key-expert roles you can fill with qualifying CVs (each scoring >= 60%)
inputFields.scoring: Each CV must generally score >= 60%; the Team Leader below 60% (or any two other experts below 60%) causes rejection, so covering all roles above the floor is essential.

criteria.label: Approach, Methodology & Work Plan
criteria.description: Quality and project-specificity of the technical proposal: understanding of the Terms of Reference, technical approach and methodology (surveys, engineering, feasibility/financial modelling, statutory/environmental strategy as applicable), and an activity-wise work plan and timeline consistent with the deliverables.

scoringGuidance.meaning: Deep, site-specific understanding of the port/waterway scope with a well-justified methodology (surveys, engineering, feasibility/financial modelling) and a realistic activity-wise work plan.
Sound methodology tailored to the assignment with a coherent work plan and deliverables schedule.
Generic but acceptable methodology with a basic work plan.
Boilerplate approach with weak or missing work plan / understanding of the TOR.

inputFields.label: How tailored is your methodology to this specific port/waterway assignment?
inputFields.scoring: Evaluators reward demonstrated understanding of the specific site (bathymetry, dredging, traffic, connectivity, environmental constraints); fully project-specific approaches score in the top band. VOCPA splits this block: Understanding of TOR 20%, Methodology 50%, Work plan 30%.

inputFields.label: Do you have a detailed activity-wise work plan and timeline matched to the deliverables?
inputFields.scoring: A clear, realistic work plan consistent with the TOR carries dedicated marks (e.g. 30% of the methodology block in the VOCPA model).

criteria.label: Technical Presentation
criteria.description: A presentation of the proposed approach, methodology and team by the consultant's key personnel to the evaluation committee, used in several major-port RFPs as a distinct scored criterion (and, in advisory RFPs, as 'team interaction').

scoringGuidance.meaning: Confident, well-structured presentation by the actual proposed team demonstrating command of the project and answering committee questions convincingly.
Clear presentation covering approach, methodology and team with adequate responses.
Basic presentation with limited depth or partial team participation.
Weak or generic presentation, or key personnel unable to attend/respond.

inputFields.label: Can your proposed key team present the approach to the evaluation committee?
inputFields.scoring: Several port RFPs (e.g. VOCPA) allocate dedicated marks (e.g. 10 of 100) to a presentation; advisory RFPs score 'team interaction' similarly.

inputFields.label: Will the presentation be delivered by the actually proposed key personnel (not a sales team)?
inputFields.scoring: Committees score higher when the named Team Leader and specialists present, evidencing genuine availability and command of the assignment.

keyPolicies.name: Manual for Procurement of Consultancy & Other Services, Ministry of Finance (Updated June 2022)
keyPolicies.relevance: Defines the systems of consultant selection (QCBS, QBS, LCS, FBS, CQS, SSS), the two-stage evaluation (responsiveness, quality, cost), the Consultancy Evaluation Committee and the QCBS combined-score formula that major-port and IWAI consultancy RFPs follow.

keyPolicies.name: General Financial Rules (GFR) 2017 - Rule 192 (QCBS) & Rule 193 (LCS)
keyPolicies.relevance: Establishes QCBS as the default for quality-sensitive consultancy, requires the RFP to specify the minimum qualifying technical score and the technical:financial weights, and caps the non-financial (technical) weight at 80% - explaining why 80:20 is the highest ratio used by port authorities.

keyPolicies.name: IICA - QCBS Illustration (Indian Institute of Corporate Affairs)
keyPolicies.relevance: Worked example of the QCBS mechanics used across Indian government consultancy tenders: technical grading bands, the minimum qualifying technical score (>= 70 in the illustration), conversion of price to financial score, and the 70:30 combined-score computation.

keyPolicies.name: Sagarmala - National Perspective Plan & Techno-Economic Feasibility Reports (MoPSW)
keyPolicies.relevance: The Sagarmala programme drives a large share of port-sector consultancy demand (master plans, TEFRs and DPRs for new/expanded ports such as Vadhavan, Sagar Island, Dugarajapatnam, Colachel, Sirkazhi and Belekeri), setting the policy context and typical scope for feasibility and DPR consultancies.

keyPolicies.name: World Bank - Procurement Plan, Jal Marg Vikas Project (IWAI / Ministry of Shipping)
keyPolicies.relevance: Documents the suite of IWAI inland-waterway consultancies on National Waterway-1 (detailed feasibility & engineering, ESIA/EMP/RAP, IWT sector strategy, ferry services, asset management, technical support services), all selected under QCBS with awarded values - the reference set for inland-waterway consultant selection.
